summary: Verified that TodaysEvents now shows a visible fallback message when an error occurs instead of disappearing, while the normal loading and no-events behaviors remain unchanged and existing build and smoke QA still pass.
checks_run: Reviewed TodaysEvents.tsx implementation for error handling and fallback rendering.
Confirmed from the worker result that npm run build and npm run test:e2e:smoke both succeeded after the change.
checks_passed: When an error state is set, TodaysEvents logs the error and returns a compact section with a visible fallback message instead of returning null.
The existing loading state and the behavior when there are simply no events remain unchanged, and the worker already verified build and home smoke QA still pass.
notes: This is a targeted UX hardening change for error cases: users now see a clear message when today’s events cannot be loaded, rather than the section silently disappearing. Functional behavior under normal and empty-data conditions is unchanged.
